clientPosting.title: Technical Code & Flow Audit: E-Commerce Apparel Customizer (React/JS)
clientPosting.summary: E-commerce apparel brand seeking technical audit of interactive design customizer. Microsoft Clarity logs show: (1) dead clicks & JavaScript errors ('undefined is not an object') blocking customization interactions; (2) high homepage bounce rates due to heavy script-load blocking.
requirements: Diagnose root causes of JS errors and state management failures in customizer
Analyze homepage asset-loading flow to isolate bounce triggers
Provide direct code fixes OR prioritized Technical Remediation Roadmap

michaelsVision.demoGoal: Full-featured bowling shirt e-commerce store showcasing high-quality interactive apparel customizer

heroFeatureDetail.3dVisualization: Photorealistic 3D shirt model (Owayo-quality fabric shading, proper form, NOT flat extrude) with orbit controls, rotate/zoom
heroFeatureDetail.patternLibrary: 6+ pre-loaded patterns OR photorealistic image upload (PNG/JPG wrap full shirt: front + back)
heroFeatureDetail.imageFrontBack: Separate upload slots for shirt front and back (can be same or different); independent alignment per face
heroFeatureDetail.smartAlignment: Auto-align uploaded images: deterministic pixel-scan (instant) + optional WebGPU vision model (progressive enhancement) to center & scale image onto shirt
heroFeatureDetail.manualAlignment: Zoom/scale, X offset, Y offset, rotation sliders per face; real-time preview on 3D model
heroFeatureDetail.liveTextureApplication: Three.js material swaps applied instantly with texture transforms
heroFeatureDetail.colorCustomization: Per-segment color picker (base, collar, cuffs independently) when no image uploaded
heroFeatureDetail.textOnBack: Font/color/size picker, canvas-to-texture, applied to back UV region

michaelsVision.supportingFeature1Detail: Size selector (S/M/L/XL/2XL), quantity, Add to Cart; localStorage-persisted cart with live customization thumbnail
michaelsVision.supportingFeature2Detail: Side-by-side comparison: client's error pattern vs. this demo's architecture solutions (answers job posting directly)

frontend.rationale: Static self-contained app, no Node server required for GH Pages deployment. Next.js mentioned in orchestrator as Michael's desired pattern; Vite matches the static hosting model.
frontend.stateManagement: Zustand (avoids 'undefined is not a function' class bugs client experienced)
frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS (production-quality, industry-fit, zero michaelwegter.com tokens)
frontend.fonts: Google Fonts (Inter for UI, Bebas Neue / Black Han Sans for display)

backend.architecture: PHP proxy pattern (architectural narrative) + Flask API
backend.auth: JWT (existing mw-backend auth), seeded demo account for login flow

designDirection.vibe: Retro-modern bowling alley — 1960s Americana meets clean SaaS product. Bold, fun, confident.
designDirection.typography: Bebas Neue / Black Han Sans (display), Inter (body/UI)
designDirection.layout: Full-width hero + 3D canvas front-center, sidebar customizer, product details below
designDirection.mobileResponsive: Canvas collapses to portrait, controls below

featureList.name: 3D Interactive Customizer
featureList.description: Photorealistic shirt model (fabric shading, proper 3D form like owayo.com) with orbit controls; 6+ pattern library OR full-wrap image upload (front+back separate, independent alignment); smart auto-align (deterministic + optional WebGPU) + manual zoom/scale/offset/rotation sliders; live preview; color picker (base/collar/cuffs); text-on-back with font/color/size

featureList.name: Product Page + Cart
featureList.description: Size selector, quantity, Add to Cart; localStorage cart persistence; customization thumbnail

featureList.name: Audit Credibility Section
featureList.description: Side-by-side: client's error pattern vs. demo's architecture solutions (dead-click prevention, performance optimization)

productionQuality: Performance: code-split Three.js, lazy-load canvas only when active (proves homepage load optimization)
Smooth scroll, subtle entrance animations (CSS transitions, no heavy lib)
hardRequirements: Shirt MUST look visually 3D with depth and fabric realism (photorealistic like owayo.com), NOT flat extruded shape
Image upload to front MUST wrap around and cover front face (upload → shirt updates live)
Image upload to back MUST show independently on back face when rotated (separate from front)
Auto-fit/smart-align button MUST visibly re-center and scale image on click
Alignment sliders (zoom, X/Y offset, rotation) MUST update 3D rendering in real time (< 100ms lag)
Color picker MUST still work when no image is uploaded (solid color mode)
Pattern presets MUST still work when no image is uploaded
Text-on-back MUST be visible on 3D model
Zero console errors on first paint
Fast homepage load (deferred canvas proves performance expertise)
Cover letter addresses Microsoft Clarity / dead-click diagnosis by name
criticalPathRisks: 3D realism: free bowling shirt GLTF may not exist; fallback to procedurally-generated CylinderGeometry with half-shells for front/back separation (faster, safer than URL hunting)
Front/back split requires clean UV mapping per face (seam z-fighting at theta=0/PI boundary) — use 5-degree overlap to avoid visual artifacts
Smart alignment deterministic pixel-scan must be instant (<50ms) for UX; WebGPU segmentation model is async/lazy (50MB download) and optional only
Texture transforms via THREE.Texture matrix (rotation, repeat, offset) must re-apply on every slider change; use useMemo keyed on transform values to avoid redundant recalc
Three.js UV texture mapping for name-on-back requires known UV layout; may need custom UV model or canvas-overlay
PHP proxy is architecture narrative only; real implementation is Flask-only (PHP layer described in proposal)

scopeFence.touchpoints: customizer → product page → cart (no checkout/payment processing)

nextSteps: Planner: lock concrete build plan with design tokens, feature scope, Vite+Three.js architecture
Researcher: find free bowling/short-sleeve shirt GLTF; source 6+ retro pattern assets; reference three.js shirt-customizer examples
Demo-builder: scaffold Vite+React+Three.js; build 3D canvas + customizer panel; add Flask blueprint to mw-backend
Deploy: push both repos, verify live
Deploy-test: Playwright flow (rotate, swap pattern, add text, cart)
Proposal-writer: emphasize audit skills + live demo; lead with Clarity/dead-click familiarity

handoff.produced: brief.json v2 (improve pass 1) — integrated new 3D realism + image upload + smart alignment requirements into hero feature; added front/back image separation + alignment controls + auto-fit button as hard gates
handoff.decisions: Image upload now primary (not just patterns); front+back separate with independent transforms; smart alignment = deterministic pixel-scan (instant) + optional WebGPU (lazy, progressive); CylinderGeometry fallback (no GLTF dependency); fabric shading + normals for photorealism (owayo-level not flat extrude)
handoff.nextNeeds: Demo-builder: edit ShirtCanvas.tsx for CylinderGeometry half-shells + fabric material; add smartAlign.ts (pixel-scan logic); update OptionsPanel.tsx with tabbed front/back upload + transform sliders; test front/back upload + alignment controls + real-time preview
handoff.risks: Seam z-fighting on cylinder split; texture matrix updates must be keyed on transform state (useMemo); deterministic align must stay <50ms; WebGPU model size (lazy import only)
